title: Marine and waterways transport operatives
description: Attend to live farm, ranch, open range or aquacultural animals that may include cattle, sheep, swine, goats, horses and other equines, poultry, rabbits, finfish, shellfish, and bees. Attend to animals produced for animal products, such as meat, fur, skins, feathers, eggs, milk, and honey. Duties may include feeding, watering, herding, grazing, milking, castrating, branding, de-beaking, weighing, catching, and loading animals. May maintain records on animals; examine animals to detect diseases and injuries; assist in birth deliveries; and administer medications, vaccinations, or insecticides as appropriate. May clean and maintain animal housing areas. Includes workers who shear wool from sheep and collect eggs in hatcheries. Hunt, trap, catch, or gather wild animals or aquatic animals and plants. May use nets, traps, or other equipment. May haul catch onto ship or other vessel. Assemble, install, repair, or maintain electric or hydraulic freight or passenger elevators, escalators, or dumbwaiters. (Based on 30 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Issue directions for loading, unloading, and seating in boats, and secure boats to docks with mooring lines and cast off lines for departure.
Attach hoses and operate pumps to transfer substances to and from liquid cargo tanks, and maintain desired courses using navigational aids.
Maintain a ship's engines under the direction of engineering officers, and perform general marine vessel maintenance or repair work as needed.
Monitor engine and machinery indicators when vessels are underway, reporting abnormalities and ensuring safety through established procedures.
Direct safety operations in emergency situations and participate in emergency drills as required while maintaining government-issued certifications.
Operate engine throttles and steering mechanisms to guide boats on desired courses, and steer vessels while monitoring navigational instruments.
Clean and polish decks and maintain ship equipment, including loading and unloading materials, vehicles, or passengers from vessels.
Examine machinery to verify pressure or lubricant flows, monitor and test operations of engines, and ensure compliance with safety codes.
Inspect, maintain, and repair equipment, machinery, and structures, including cleaning stalls, pens, and organizing the activities of crew members.
Herd livestock to grazing pastures, monitor food and water supplies, and provide medical treatment such as administering medications and vaccinations.
Lubricate wearing surfaces of mechanical parts using oils or other lubricants.
Disassemble and repair mechanical control devices or valves, using power tools, hand tools, and cutting torches.
Order replacement parts and maintain stocks of parts used in on-site installation, maintenance, and repair of appliances.
Record maintenance information including test results, material usage, and repairs made, while maintaining log books that detail repairs and checks performed.
Test and examine gas pipelines and equipment to locate leaks and faulty connections and determine the pressure and flow of gas.
Inspect and test operation, mechanical parts, and circuitry of signal systems, gates, and signals to ensure proper operation and safety compliance.
Clean and reinstall parts, clean internal compartments, and maintain equipment by removing dirt, scale, and rust.
Install, inspect, maintain, and repair a variety of appliances, such as refrigerators, washing machines, and stoves, ensuring proper functionality.
Respond to emergency calls for problems such as gas leaks and locate malfunctions in equipment and appliances using diagnostic tools.
Test newly installed equipment to ensure it meets specifications and make adjustments necessary for proper operation.
Participate in training to keep skills up to date and stay informed of new products and developments in the field.
Drive motor vehicles to job sites and collect payment upon job completion for services rendered.
Assemble, install, repair, and maintain elevators, escalators and related equipment using hand and power tools, and conduct tests to ensure performance.
Use precision testing equipment to test valves and regulators for leaks and accurate temperature and pressure settings.
Make adjustments to meter components and investigate illegal tapping into service lines, ensuring compliance with regulations.
Prepare documents, such as work orders and invoices, including providing customers with cost estimates for equipment installation.
Test electrical circuits and components as well as troubleshoot and diagnose issues based on wiring diagrams and equipment manuals.
Install, repair, and maintain electrical wiring and connections in appliances, including making adjustments as necessary for optimal performance.
Inspect switch-controlling mechanisms and tightness of connections, using test equipment as required to ensure safe operation of equipment.
Connect regulators to test stands and adjust settings to meet specifications, ensuring equipment operates within required parameters.
Show customers how to maintain equipment.
Dismantle engines, using hand tools, and examine parts for defects, then remove engines from equipment for repair.
Repair, replace, or maintain engines and defective parts such as magnetos, gears, and carburetors, using hand tools.
Perform routine maintenance such as cleaning, lubricating, and tuning parts to ensure proper operation of engines and equipment.
Test and inspect engines and electrical systems to diagnose malfunctions, verify repairs, and determine required fixes using diagnostic instruments.
Record repairs made, time spent, and parts used, and maintain equipment logs that detail performance issues.
Reassemble engines and equipment after repairs and maintenance, testing them to ensure proper operation thereafter.
Diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs, using diagnostic equipment and consulting manuals or schematics.
Clean, lubricate, and perform routine maintenance on machinery and vehicles, such as tractors and harvesters.
Transport machine parts, tools, and equipment between work areas and perform assembly, installation, or repair as part of a team.
Clean machines and machine parts using cleaning solvents, cloths, air guns, hoses, vacuums, or other equipment.
Replace or repair lining in machines or equipment compartments or containers.
Dismantle and disassemble machines and equipment to remove parts for repair, using tools such as hand tools, chain falls, or hoists.
Repair or replace broken or malfunctioning components of machinery or equipment.
Perform maintenance and repair on machinery or equipment to ensure proper operation.
Inspect and test equipment or components to diagnose malfunctions and identify repair needs.

Analyze test results and machine error messages to diagnose problems in equipment.
Monitor employee performance and provide training in safety, repair techniques, or equipment use.
Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work.
Confer with customers or personnel to resolve problems or exchange information.
